Article Title: SC: Officials of 3 states liable for Chambal mining
Article Text: NEW DELHI : Taking suo motu cognisance of destruction of wildlife habitats in the National Chambal Sanctuary, Supreme Court on Friday issued notice to the govts of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh and observed that their officials should be made liable for the damage as they failed to prevent illegal sand mining in the area.A bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta expressed concern over the damage being done to the sanctuary and said all aspects had to be examined.“The officials of the departments concerned, that is, forest, mining and water resources as well as police authorities of the states of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh by their lethargy and inaction would be liable to be held vicariously liable for aiding and abetting the destruction of these precious habitats by allowing illegal sand mining to continue,” the bench said.
Article Summary: Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta expressed concern over the damage being done to the sanctuary. “The officials of the departments concerned, that is, forest, mining and water resources as well as police authorities of the states of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh by their lethargy and inaction would be liable to be held vicariously liable,” the bench said.

Article Title: Omar Abdullah: Sweeping transfers by EC only happen in non-BJP-governed states: Omar
Article Text:
Omar Abdullah (File photo)

NEW DELHI: J&K CM and National Conference leader Omar Abdullah on Friday came out strongly in support of his West Bengal counterpart Mamata Banerjee on the issue of Election Commission transferring officials in poll-bound Bengal, saying these “sweeping transfers only happen in non-BJP-governed states”.“Bengal will once again prove that officers don’t win elections for political parties, the leaders of political parties do,” said Omar, an INDIA bloc ally, reacting to Mamata’s Thursday post on X in which she alleged that the manner in which EC has “singled out and targeted Bengal is not just unprecedented but deeply alarming”.Even before the formal notification of elections, more than 50 senior officials, including chief secretary, home secretary and DGP, were “summarily and arbitrarily removed”, she alleged.Sharing Mamata’s post, Omar said, “No amount of effort by EC to gerrymander will change the results. Come counting day Mamata didi will win a thumping majority.”Highlighting that various parties have come forward to support Mamata, her party TMC shared a video clip of SP chief Akhilesh Yadav, while talking to reporters, saying, “I want to congratulate respected Mamata Banerjee in advance and said the people of Bengal will vote in her favour.” He said that the conspiracy led by BJP to defeat her, including the recent transfer of officials, will not work.“It is people who vote not the officials,” Akhilesh said.On Thursday, after Mamata’s post, AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal had posted, “BJP has turned EC into its weapon to win elections through deceit. What is happening today in Bengal is exactly what happened in the Delhi elections as well.” “Names were struck off the voter list, the police administration gave free rein to BJP’s thuggery, and the entire administration was engaged in ensuring BJP’s victory. The very fabric of democracy was torn apart,” he added.AAP, which is not part of the INDIA bloc, had signed the opposition’s notice, piloted by TMC, seeking a motion for removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar that was submitted to both Houses of Parliament last week.Shiv Sena (UBT)’s Sanjay Raut said on X that “defeating Mamata didi in West Bengal is not just difficult, it’s impossible! That’s why BJP is playing dirty by hiding behind the shield of EC”.
Article Summary: J&K CM Omar Abdullah came out strongly in support of his West Bengal counterpart Mamata Banerjee on the issue of Election Commission transferring officials in poll-bound Bengal. “Bengal will once again prove that officers don’t win elections for political parties, the leaders of political parties do,” said Omar.

Article Title: 'Denied but not defeated': Congress leader Shama Mohamed flags fewer women candidates in Kerala poll list
Article Text:
Congress' spokesperson Shama Mohamed

Watch 'BJP A Zero Seat Party': Shashi Tharoor's BIG Claim On Kerala Polls, Bats For Congress Comeback 'BJP A Zero Seat Party': Shashi Tharoor's BIG Claim On Kerala Polls, Bats For Congress Comeback

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Shama Mohamed on Friday raised concerns over the low representation of women in the party's candidate list for the upcoming Kerala assembly elections, urging Rahul Gandhi to intervene.In a post on X, Mohamed said that out of 92 tickets, only nine had been allotted to women. She also pointed to the Lok Sabha elections, where just one woman was fielded among 16 candidates in Kerala. "Denied but not defeated," she wrote, appealing to Rahul Gandhi to support women leaders in the state unit.Her remarks come a day after the Congress finalised its candidates. Leader of opposition VD Satheesan said the party has locked in all 95 nominees it is contesting. "We had announced 55 candidates earlier and have now finalised the remaining 40. The selection process is complete, and we will now move into campaign mode," he said.Kerala will vote in a single phase on April 9, with counting scheduled for May 4.
Article Summary: Congress leader Shama Mohamed raises concerns over the low representation of women in the party's candidate list for the upcoming Kerala assembly elections. Out of 92 tickets, only nine had been allotted to women. She also pointed to the Lok Sabha elections, where just one woman was fielded among 16 candidates.

Article Title: Kerala polls: Congress releases 2nd list, MP Sudhakaran agrees to stay away
Article Text:
Watch Nemom in Focus: Can BJP Convert Stronghold into Kerala Foothold? Nemom in Focus: Can BJP Convert Stronghold into Kerala Foothold?

Poll Do you agree with Congress's decision to not field any sitting MP in Kerala assembly polls? Yes, it's a good strategy No, it may backfire

THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: Ending days of uncertainty, Congress on Thursday released its second list of 37 candidates in Kerala, completing the selection process for all 95 seats the party will contest in the state. In line with the directive from the party high command, no sitting MP has been fielded in the assembly polls.The names featured in the second list included Mohammad Shiyas in Kochi, Sandeep Varrier in Trikaripur, Abin Varkey in Aranmula, K S Sabarinadhan in Nemom, and T O Mohanan in Kannur.The selection process, however, was not without internal differences. Amid indications that the party may field his critic and former mayor Mohanan from Kannur, former KPCC president K Sudhakaran had made his displeasure known, even indicating that he may consider entering the fray as an Independent candidate.On Thursday, however, Sudhakaran ruled out contesting as an Independent from Kannur, asserting he would remain loyal to the party. Sources indicated Sudhakaran softened his stand after senior Congress' leader A K Antony stepped in to defuse the crisis and party president Mallikarjun Kharge personally intervened, reaching out to the former state chief and urging restraint.In Kochi, Deepti Mary Varghese was among the frontrunners, but the leadership backed the nominee supported by leader of the opposition, V D Satheesan.
Article Summary: Congress on Thursday released its second list of 37 candidates in Kerala. No sitting MP has been fielded in the assembly polls. Names featured in the second list included Mohammad Shiyas in Kochi, Sandeep Varrier in Trikaripur, Abin Varkey in Aranmula.

Article Title: TMC booth chief killed, kin claim factional feud
Article Text:
Representative image

A 38-year-old TMC booth chief was found murdered at Minakhan in Bengal's North 24 Parganas on Thursday. There were signs of torture on his body. His family claimed faction feuds within party led to his killing. Locals came across Kazi Masiur Rahman's body, wrapped in a blanket, along Haroa-Bhangar road. Preliminary findings suggest he was hacked to death, but only a postmortem will confirm the exact cause, police said.
Article Summary: Kazi Masiur Rahman's body was found wrapped in a blanket. His family claimed faction feuds within party led to his killing. Preliminary findings suggest he was hacked to death, but only a postmortem will confirm the exact cause.

Article Title: BJP Bengal 2nd list: Backs old hands, ditches GNLF
Article Text:
Watch Battle For Bhabanipur: BJP’s Suvendu Adhikari Challenges Mamata Banerjee, Warns Nandigram Repeat Battle For Bhabanipur: BJP’s Suvendu Adhikari Challenges Mamata Banerjee, Warns Nandigram Repeat

KOLKATA/DARJEELING: BJP released its second list of 111 candidates for Bengal, placing faith in familiar faces - former MPs, sitting MLAs and veterans - while ditching long-time ally Gorkha National Liberation Front in three seats of Darjeeling-Kalimpong hills, report Rohit Khanna & Roshan Gupta.Former MPs Nisith Pramanik (Mathabhanga), Arjun Singh (Noapara), Roopa Ganguly (Sonarpur Dakshin) and Dibyendu Adhikari (Egra) made the cut, as did Ashok Kirtania (Bongaon Uttar), Swapan Majumdar (Bongaon Dakshin) and Subrata Thakur (Gaighata) in a list of eight sitting MLAs.Other prominent names to receive nominations include Ritesh Tiwari (Kashipur-Belgachhia), Priyanka Tibrewal (Entally) and Sanjay Singh (Bally).Former IPS officer Rajesh Kumar, whom the Election Commission had appointed Kolkata Police commissioner for 50 days during the 2019 Lok Sabha polls, got the ticket for Jagatdal. The 1990-batch officer retired as principal secretary of the dept of mass education and library services.BJP’s second list has 14 women, taking the total so far to 25. Rekha Patra, the face of BJP’s Sandeshkhali agitation against arrested and suspended Trinamool strongman Sheikh Shahjahan, will contest from Hingalganj.In the hills, prospective candidates from ally GNLF weren’t picked for any of the three constituencies – Darjeeling, Kurseong and Kalimpong. The Subhash Ghising-founded party has been an NDA constituent for a decade but has since been delisted by EC, leaving its aspiring candidates dependent on BJP.GNLF’s secretary general Neeraj Zimba won Darjeeling in the 2021 polls as a BJP nominee. Previously, he won a 2019 bypoll on the lotus symbol.BJP nominated former Indian hockey captain Bharat Chetri for Kalimpong and accommodated GNLF’s rival Gorkha Janmukti Morcha in Darjeeling, fielding its youth central general secretary Noman Rai. The Kurseong ticket went to Sonam Lama, the local BJP block president.GNLF central panel spokesperson Y Lama said his party wasn’t even consulted. “Our central committee will decide the future course of action.”Kharagpur Sadar MLA Hiran Chattopadhyay, whose seat had been allotted to former state unit chief Dilip Ghosh, has been fielded in Shyampur. Tapas Roy, who won Maniktala on a Trinamool ticket in 2021 and defected to BJP ahead of unsuccessfully contesting Kolkata North in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls, has been fielded in his old constituency. Roopa Ganguly has been fielded from Sonarpur Dakshin.On the Kurmi front, BJP decided to support independent candidate Biswajit Mahato in Joypur, Purulia. In its first list, the party had nominated Rajesh Mahato, former Kurmi Samaj state president, from Gopiballavpur. Both Rajesh and Ajit have been pushing for ST status for the Kurmi community and inclusion of Kurmali in the Eighth Schedule.
Article Summary: Former MPs Nisith Pramanik (Mathabhanga), Arjun Singh (Noapara), Roopa Ganguly (Sonarpur Dakshin) and Dibyendu Adhikari (Egra) made the cut. Former IPS officer Rajesh Kumar, whom the Election Commission had appointed Kolkata Police commissioner for 50 days during 2019 Lok Sabha polls, got the ticket for Jagatdal.

Article Title: AINRC, BJP sign poll pact for Puducherry election; AIADMK, LJK get 2 seats each
Article Text: Puducherry: All India NR Congress (AINRC) and BJP finalised a seat-sharing arrangement in Puducherry on Friday, ending the tussle over over inclusion of Jose Charles Martin's Latchiya Jananayaga Katchi (LJK) in the alliance.BJP managed to convince the AINRC on accommodating LJK. As per the arrangement, AINRC would contest in 16 seats while BJP and its allies will contest the remaining 14 seats. BJP has planned to contest in 10 seats, allotting two seats each to AIADMK and LJK from its quota.AINRC founder and chief minister N Rangasamy , who opposed the inclusion of LJK in the NDA fold, finally gave in after direct intervention from the BJP high command in New Delhi.He called on Union minister and BJP election in-charge for Puducherry, Mansukh Mandaviya, at a hotel to finalise the deal.Rangasamy told reporters in the presence of Mandaviya after the meeting, "We are firmly in the NDA. There is no change in our stance. We will face this election together."On the friction over the LJK, Rangasamy said Union home minister Amit Shah had personally advised him LJK's inclusion would strengthen the coalition."Based on that advice, the new party is now part of the alliance," he said.Mandaviya said Rangasamy was the leader of the NDA in Puducherry and the election will be fought under his leadership. On AINRC's demand for statehood for Puducherry, Mandaviya said NDA's poll manifesto would give an answer to the issue. He said Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Shah will visit Puducherry to spearhead the poll campaign. Rangasamy also filed nomination papers to contest from two constituencies, Thattanchavady and Mangalam.Meanwhile, the DMK- Congress front was yet to finalise seat sharing.
Article Summary: AINRC founder and chief minister N Rangasamy gave in after direct intervention from the BJP high command in New Delhi. As per the arrangement, AINRC would contest in 16 seats while BJP and its allies will contest the remaining 14 seats.

Article Title: Joe Burrow’s late-night outing with Olivia Ponton raises eyebrows just days after high-profile party appearances with Alix Earle
Article Text: Joe Burrow’s late-night outing with Olivia Ponton raises eyebrows just days after high-profile party appearances with Alix Earle
Article Summary: Joe Burrow’s late-night outing with Olivia Ponton raises eyebrows just days after high-profile party appearances with Alix Earle. Burrow and Ponton have been dating for a few months, but have never been romantically linked.
